binaries.title: clfs.sys (Common Log File System) - Storage sector-size alignment for log-block MDLs made unconditional by removing a feature-staging gate (no security-relevant change)

changed_functions.name: CClfsRequest::ReserveAndAppendLog (sub_1C0055BEC)
key_changes: Removed a feature-staging gate: unpatched calls EvaluateCurrentState (sub_1C000CE64) on the feature descriptor g_Feature_2160550201_59010878; when it returns 0 the code uses a hardcoded 512-byte round ((size+0x1ff)&0xfffffe00) for the MDL byte length, otherwise it calls CClfsRequest::GetAlignedBufferSize (sub_1C0027860) which rounds up to the device's real logical sector size. Patched removes the gate and the 512-round branch and always calls GetAlignedBufferSize.

This length only sizes an MDL built over a pre-existing log-block buffer (rdx = *(this+0x30)); it does not size any allocation. GetAlignedBufferSize rounds UP, so the removed fallback produced a length <= the aligned length. The gated-off path therefore described fewer bytes of the buffer, not more. No out-of-bounds write or read is reachable, in either direction.

CClfsLogFcbCommon::ReportFlushFailure (sub_1C000FE44) reason argument is NOT an out-of-bounds write and did not change behavior: it is a small enum constant (5 or 8). The forms rendered as MappedSystemVa+8 / &MDL->Next+5 are a decompilation artifact. Each such call sits on an error branch guarded by test <reg>,<reg>; jnz <success> (with <reg> the just-failed allocation result), so on the taken branch the base register is provably NULL and lea ecx,[rax+8] loads the literal 8 (or lea ecx,[rax+5] loads 5). Both builds emit the same mix of lea ecx,[reg+N] (reg NULL) and mov ecx,N; the index is always in bounds.
The 8-byte stack-frame difference between the two builds is incidental register/spill allocation, not a security change.
changed_functions.security_notes: No demonstrable security impact. The change removes a WIL feature-staging gate so the MDL byte length is always rounded up to the storage device's real logical sector size via CClfsRequest::GetAlignedBufferSize (sub_1C0027860) instead of a smaller fallback. The length only controls how many bytes of an already-allocated log-block buffer an MDL describes; the removed fallback used the smaller length, so it cannot describe or write past the buffer. Making the larger aligned length unconditional is safe because the buffer accommodates a full sector-aligned block, which also shows the fallback was in bounds. The CClfsLogFcbCommon::ReportFlushFailure (sub_1C000FE44) reason arguments are small in-bounds enum constants (5 and 8), identical in both builds; the pointer-looking forms are a decompilation artifact on NULL-base error branches.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
Unpatched CClfsRequest::ReserveAndAppendLog (sub_1C0055BEC) length choice:
00000001C0056379  lea     rcx, g_Feature_2160550201_59010878_FeatureDescriptorDetails
00000001C0056380  call    ?EvaluateCurrentState@@YAHPEBUreg_FeatureDescriptor@@@Z
00000001C0056385  test    eax, eax
00000001C0056387  jz      short loc_1C0056398          ; state==1 -> 512-byte round
00000001C0056389  mov     edx, ebx
00000001C005638B  mov     rcx, rsi
00000001C005638E  call    ?GetAlignedBufferSize@CClfsRequest@@AEAAKK@Z
00000001C0056393  mov     r8d, eax
00000001C0056396  jmp     short loc_1C00563A6
00000001C0056398  lea     r8d, [rbx+1FFh]
00000001C005639F  and     r8d, 0FFFFFE00h
00000001C00563A6  lea     rax, [rsp+268h+Mdl]
00000001C00563AE  mov     rdx, [rsi+30h]              ; pre-existing buffer object

Patched (gate and 512-round removed):
00000001C0056398  mov     edx, ebx
00000001C005639A  mov     rcx, r14
00000001C005639D  call    ?GetAlignedBufferSize@CClfsRequest@@AEAAKK@Z
00000001C00563A2  mov     r8d, eax
00000001C00563A5  mov     rdx, [r14+30h]

ReportFlushFailure (sub_1C000FE44) reason argument is a small enum, NOT an OOB index (unpatched):
00000001C0055F71  test    rax, rax
00000001C0055F74  jnz     short loc_1C0055FB6         ; taken on success; NOT taken here so rax == 0
00000001C0055F7B  lea     ecx, [rax+5]               ; rax == 0 => ecx = 5 (reason enum)
00000001C0055F7E  call    ?ReportFlushFailure@CClfsLogFcbCommon@@SAXW4_CLFS_FLUSH_FAILURE@@J@Z
The patched build emits the same mix of lea ecx,[reg+N] (reg NULL) and mov ecx,N at these sites; behavior identical.

changed_functions.name: CClfsRequest::WriteRestart (sub_1C0057598)
key_changes: Removed a feature-staging gate: unpatched calls EvaluateCurrentState (sub_1C000CE64) on g_Feature_2160550201_59010878; when it returns 0 the code uses the raw size from the log block header (this+0x108, from r15+0x8) as the MDL byte length, otherwise it calls CClfsRequest::GetAlignedBufferSize (sub_1C0027860). Patched removes the gate and the raw-size branch and always calls GetAlignedBufferSize.

Both the aligned branch (0x1C00576F5) and the raw branch (0x1C005771D) load the MDL buffer pointer from the same object field ([reg+70h]) and converge on the same ClfsProbeAndAllocateMdl (sub_1C0010890) at 0x1C0057735; only the length in r8d differs, and the raw value is the smaller of the two. The size sizes an MDL over a pre-existing buffer, not an allocation.
Patched code path linearized: GetAlignedBufferSize is called unconditionally before ClfsProbeAndAllocateMdl.
changed_functions.security_notes: No demonstrable security impact. Same change as CClfsRequest::ReserveAndAppendLog (sub_1C0055BEC): a feature-staging gate around the MDL byte-length computation is removed so sector-size alignment is unconditional. The length only decides how many bytes of a pre-existing log-block buffer (the same pointer at *(this+6)+0x70 in both branches) an MDL describes; the gated-off path used the smaller (raw) length and therefore cannot describe or write past the buffer. GetAlignedBufferSize rounds up, so the patched (aligned) length is the larger, safe upper bound. The originally suspected 'undersized MDL leading to out-of-bounds pool write' is inconsistent with the code: the un-aligned path uses the smaller length.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
Unpatched CClfsRequest::WriteRestart (sub_1C0057598) gated length choice:
00000001C00576DE  lea     rcx, g_Feature_2160550201_59010878_FeatureDescriptorDetails
00000001C00576E5  call    ?EvaluateCurrentState@@YAHPEBUreg_FeatureDescriptor@@@Z
00000001C00576EA  mov     r8d, [rsi+108h]            ; raw size from log block header
00000001C00576F1  test    eax, eax
00000001C00576F3  jz      short loc_1C005771D        ; state==1 -> raw-size branch
00000001C00576F5  mov     rbx, [rsi+30h]
00000001C00576FC  mov     rcx, rsi
00000001C00576FF  call    ?GetAlignedBufferSize@CClfsRequest@@AEAAKK@Z
00000001C0057704  mov     r8d, eax
00000001C0057714  mov     rdx, [rbx+70h]            ; buffer pointer
00000001C005771B  jmp     short loc_1C0057735
00000001C005771D  mov     rax, [rsi+30h]
00000001C005772E  mov     rdx, [rax+70h]            ; SAME buffer pointer
00000001C0057735  call    ClfsProbeAndAllocateMdl   ; MDL over buffer, length = r8d

Patched CClfsRequest::WriteRestart (0x1C0057594):
00000001C00576DD  mov     edx, [rsi+108h]
00000001C00576E3  mov     rcx, rsi
00000001C00576E6  call    ?GetAlignedBufferSize@CClfsRequest@@AEAAKK@Z  ; always aligned
00000001C00576EB  mov     r8d, eax
00000001C00576FB  mov     rdx, [rbx+70h]
00000001C0057702  call    ClfsProbeAndAllocateMdl
; EvaluateCurrentState call and raw-size branch removed

security_findings.entry_point: CLFS marshalling path reached when a base log file (.blf) is opened and log/restart operations are issued from user mode
security_findings.vulnerability_class: None demonstrable (behavioral/compatibility change; not CWE-787 or CWE-125)
security_findings.description: The patch removes a WIL feature-staging gate in two CLFS marshalling functions. In the unpatched build EvaluateCurrentState (sub_1C000CE64), evaluated against the feature descriptor g_Feature_2160550201_59010878, selected the byte length used for an MDL over the log-block buffer: when it returned non-zero the code called CClfsRequest::GetAlignedBufferSize (sub_1C0027860) to round the length up to the storage device's real logical sector size, and when it returned zero the code used a smaller fallback (the raw size from the log block header in sub_1C0057598, or (size+0x1ff)&0xfffffe00 in sub_1C0055BEC). The patched build deletes the gate and both fallback branches and always calls GetAlignedBufferSize. This is not a memory-safety fix: the length only controls how many bytes of a pre-existing, already-allocated log-block buffer an MDL describes (ClfsProbeAndAllocateMdl (sub_1C0010890) performs IoAllocateMdl over a supplied VirtualAddress; it does not allocate the length). GetAlignedBufferSize rounds up, so the removed fallback produced a length less than or equal to the aligned length and described fewer bytes of the buffer, never more. Making the aligned length unconditional is the stricter behavior and is safe because the buffer accommodates a full sector-aligned block. The practical effect is correct sector-size alignment of log/restart I/O on storage whose logical sector size exceeds 512 bytes (for example 4K-native disks), a compatibility/robustness concern rather than a reachable out-of-bounds condition.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: No reachable memory-safety condition. On the unpatched build, which length branch is taken depends on the runtime state of the feature g_Feature_2160550201_59010878; when the aligned branch is not taken the MDL simply describes fewer bytes of the log-block buffer. The consequence on large-sector storage is I/O whose length is not a multiple of the true sector size, not pool corruption.
security_findings.primitive: None. The changed length sizes an MDL over a pre-existing buffer, and the gated-off path used the smaller of the two lengths. There is no undersized allocation, no oversized copy, and no attacker-controlled length reaching an allocation size.
security_findings.assembly:
Unpatched CClfsRequest::WriteRestart (sub_1C0057598) converging branches:
00000001C00576F3  jz      short loc_1C005771D        ; feature state==1 -> raw-size branch
00000001C00576FF  call    ?GetAlignedBufferSize@CClfsRequest@@AEAAKK@Z
00000001C0057704  mov     r8d, eax                  ; aligned length (larger)
00000001C0057714  mov     rdx, [rbx+70h]            ; buffer pointer
00000001C005771B  jmp     short loc_1C0057735
00000001C005771D  mov     rax, [rsi+30h]
00000001C005772E  mov     rdx, [rax+70h]            ; SAME buffer pointer
00000001C0057735  call    ClfsProbeAndAllocateMdl   ; length = r8d (raw = smaller in this branch)

Patched: gate removed, GetAlignedBufferSize unconditional before ClfsProbeAndAllocateMdl.

debugging_aids.notes: EvaluateCurrentState (sub_1C000CE64) is a feature-staging helper: it calls EvaluateFeature on the reg_FeatureDescriptor and returns (current_state != 1). GetAlignedBufferSize (sub_1C0027860) queries the storage device object (via a guarded indirect call through vtable+0xF0/+240) for the logical sector size and rounds the requested length up to it, falling back to a 512-byte round when the query fails or the sector size is <= 0x200. Symbols are from the binaries' own name records: ClfsProbeAndAllocateMdl (sub_1C0010890) = ClfsProbeAndAllocateMdl, CClfsRequest::GetAlignedBufferSize (sub_1C0027860) = CClfsRequest::GetAlignedBufferSize, EvaluateCurrentState (sub_1C000CE64) = EvaluateCurrentState, CClfsLogFcbCommon::ReportFlushFailure (sub_1C000FE44) = CClfsLogFcbCommon::ReportFlushFailure.

security_findings.vulnerability_class: None (decompilation artifact; not an out-of-bounds write)
security_findings.description: An earlier reading suggested that CClfsRequest::ReserveAndAppendLog (sub_1C0055BEC) passes kernel-mode pointers as the array index argument to CClfsLogFcbCommon::ReportFlushFailure (sub_1C000FE44). This is not correct. ReportFlushFailure(reason, status) sign-extends reason and uses it to index m_cFlushFailures (and, for reason==0xB, a clamped ring m_rcFlushFailureCodes). Every call site in ReserveAndAppendLog passes a small enum constant (5 or 8). The forms a decompiler renders as MappedSystemVa+8 or &MDL->Next+5 are an artifact: each call sits on an error branch dominated by test <reg>,<reg>; jnz <success>, where <reg> is the just-failed IoAllocateMdl / MmMapLockedPagesSpecifyCache result, so on the taken branch <reg> is provably NULL and lea ecx,[rax+8] loads the literal 8 (or lea ecx,[rax+5] loads 5). The index is always in bounds. Both the unpatched and patched builds emit the same mix of lea ecx,[reg+N] (reg NULL) and mov ecx,N at these sites; behavior is identical. There is no out-of-bounds write and no change of behavior.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Not applicable; the reason argument is always a small in-bounds enum constant in both builds.
security_findings.evidence:
Unpatched call sites in CClfsRequest::ReserveAndAppendLog (sub_1C0055BEC), each dominated by a NULL check:
00000001C0055F74  jnz     short loc_1C0055FB6        ; NOT taken here => rax == 0
00000001C0055F7B  lea     ecx, [rax+5]              ; ecx = 5
00000001C0055F7E  call    ?ReportFlushFailure@CClfsLogFcbCommon@@SAXW4_CLFS_FLUSH_FAILURE@@J@Z
00000001C0056014  jnz     short loc_1C005604D        ; NOT taken here => rax == 0
00000001C0056019  lea     ecx, [rax+8]              ; ecx = 8
00000001C005601C  call    ?ReportFlushFailure@CClfsLogFcbCommon@@SAXW4_CLFS_FLUSH_FAILURE@@J@Z
ReportFlushFailure (sub_1C000FE44) body:
00000001C000FE44  movsxd  rax, ecx                  ; small enum
00000001C000FE4E  lock inc rva m_cFlushFailures[r9+rax*4]
00000001C000FE57  cmp     ecx, 0Bh                  ; ring write only for reason==0xB
00000001C000FE77  cmova   ecx, r8d                  ; index clamped to 0x14
00000001C000FE7E  mov     rva m_rcFlushFailureCodes[r9+rax*4], edx
security_findings.assembly: See evidence. The same NULL-based lea/mov pattern is present in both builds; the reason enum values (5, 8) are unchanged.

debugging_aids.notes: CClfsLogFcbCommon::ReportFlushFailure (sub_1C000FE44) is a CLFS internal counter/telemetry logger with two static arrays (m_cFlushFailures counters and m_rcFlushFailureCodes status ring). The index is the reason enum; the ring write is reached only for reason==0xB and is clamped to index 0x14. No kernel pointer reaches the index.

summary: The patch changes exactly two CLFS marshalling functions, CClfsRequest::ReserveAndAppendLog (sub_1C0055BEC) and CClfsRequest::WriteRestart (sub_1C0057598 unpatched / sub_1C0057594 patched). Both remove a WIL feature-staging gate (EvaluateCurrentState (sub_1C000CE64) on the feature descriptor g_Feature_2160550201_59010878) that selected the byte length for an MDL built over the log-block buffer, so the length is now always rounded up to the storage device's real logical sector size via CClfsRequest::GetAlignedBufferSize (sub_1C0027860). This is not a memory-safety fix: the length only decides how many bytes of a pre-existing, already-allocated buffer an MDL describes (ClfsProbeAndAllocateMdl (sub_1C0010890) does IoAllocateMdl over a supplied VirtualAddress; it does not allocate the length). GetAlignedBufferSize rounds up, so the removed fallback (raw size in sub_1C0057598, 512-byte round in sub_1C0055BEC) used the smaller length and described fewer bytes of the buffer, never more; the patched aligned length is the larger, safe bound. The practical effect is correct sector-size alignment of log/restart I/O on storage with logical sectors larger than 512 bytes (e.g. 4K-native disks) - a compatibility/robustness change, not a reachable out-of-bounds condition. The previously suspected CClfsLogFcbCommon::ReportFlushFailure (sub_1C000FE44) 'kernel-pointer-as-index' out-of-bounds write is a decompilation artifact: the reason arguments are small in-bounds enum constants (5 and 8) on NULL-base error branches, identical in both builds. No CWE-787/CWE-125 primitive is present; severity is informational.
